text:
It is generally safe to swim after eating a meal. The old advice to wait 30 minutes to an hour is largely a myth. Most people can swim comfortably after eating, though you might experience mild stomach discomfort or cramps if you eat a very large or heavy meal and then engage in vigorous swimming right away.

If you want to be cautious, waiting 30 minutes is reasonable, especially after a particularly heavy meal. For light snacks or small meals, you can swim almost immediately. Listen to your body—if you feel comfortable, it’s fine to swim.
